title: Podman build command stopped recognizing `containers-storage` as a transport since 6.0.0
body:
### Issue Description

`podman build` no longer accepts the `containers-storage:` transport prefix in a Containerfile FROM directive starting with podman 6.0.0. This previously worked in podman 5.x. This breaks workflows that build container images on top of locally-stored bootc images, where the base image only exists in containers-storage (not in a registry)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a Containerfile which uses any local images and has the `containers-storage:` prefix
```
  cat > Containerfile <<'EOF'
  FROM containers-storage:localhost/tmt/bootc/6f348c0d-f20e-4a36-8317-dc91e0bffac8
  RUN cat /etc/os-release
  EOF
```
2. Run `podman build .`




### Describe the results you received

Actual behavior (podman 6.0.0, RHEL 10.3):
```
  Error: invalid base image specification "containers-storage:localhost/tmt/bootc/6f348c0d-f20e-4a36-8317-dc91e0bffac8": unexpected container image transport "containers-storage"
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected behavior (podman 5.8.2, RHEL 10.2):
  Build proceeds normally:
```
  STEP 1/2: FROM containers-storage:localhost/tmt/bootc/94427df9-743c-4528-b1d1-a17e06ec0e34
  ...
```

### podman info output


comments.body:
I defer to @nalind but AFAIK there was some effort to lock down what the Containerfile gets access to.

>   FROM containers-storage:localhost/tmt/bootc/6f348c0d-f20e-4a36-8317-dc91e0bffac8

For this example I am not sure why containers-storage would be needed? Just the name should work fine as it first looks into the local storage before pulling the image AFAIK.
The base image reference comes from `bootc status`, which reports `containers-storage:` as the transport. The tmt test framework uses this pattern for bootc image-mode testing.

To make it more confusing, in our case, the `podman pull` doesn't work without a defined transport. The result is the same in both podman 6.0.0 and 5.8.2
```
# podman pull localhost/tmt/bootc/d29ff620-4914-4d78-a779-87039f6c5544
Trying to pull localhost/tmt/bootc/d29ff620-4914-4d78-a779-87039f6c5544:latest...
WARN[0000] Failed, retrying in 1s ... (1/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/tmt/bootc/d29ff620-4914-4d78-a779-87039f6c5544:latest: fetching manifest latest in localhost/tmt/bootc/d29ff620-4914-4d78-a779-87039f6c5544: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused

#podman pull containers-storage:localhost/tmt/bootc/d29ff620-4914-4d78-a779-87039f6c5544
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 22d349b1759d skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 22d349b1759d skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a8ebb9e288ea skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 40e13358c33c skipped: already exists  
Copying blob cfa146b69f19 skipped: already exists
...
```

podman pull will always try to pull an image, that is the point of the command. However commands like podman run, or buildah build should AFAIK reuse a local image before trying to pull that (depends on the pull policy set for the command)
PR with proposed change:  https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/pull/29247.  Once merged, we will need to tag and release
